input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Ensemble UQ calibrated on in-distribution configurations while the COU samples novel configurations the ensemble has not been calibrated against.

case_context.cou: the potential drives a reaction-rate estimate over transition states
case_context.decision_consequence: reaction pathway selected on the estimate

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Calibration was measured on near-equilibrium configurations; the COU samples transition states from a different distribution where the ensemble is uncalibrated, so the test-split ECE does not bound the COU's uncertainty.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the potential's transition-state forces are wrong (a verdict the evidence does not support)
treats equilibrium-split calibration as calibration for transition states

hardness.obvious_signal: the ensemble's force-error calibration looks good on the held-out test split
hardness.conflicting_signal: the held-out split is drawn from the same equilibrium configurations; the COU samples high-energy transition states (a different distribution) where ML interatomic-potential ensembles are known to be overconfident

label_provenance.external_grounding: BNN vs deep ensembles for UQ in ML interatomic potentials (arXiv:2509.19180): ensemble UQ is distribution-dependent and overconfident off-distribution.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
